name: Middletown State Bank
description_short: Community bank serving Middletown, Greenview, and Latham, Illinois since 1917, offering personal and business checking, savings, loans, and online banking services.
description_long:
Middletown State Bank was founded in 1917 as the Marbold State Bank of Middletown and continues to operate as a community-focused financial institution. The bank maintains three physical branch locations across Illinois in Middletown, Greenview, and Latham, positioning itself as a convenient local banking option for the communities it serves.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of personal and business banking services including checking and savings accounts, debit cards, personal and business loans, online banking, mobile banking, and financial calculators. Customers can access these services through multiple channels: in-person at branch locations, via online banking platforms, through mobile applications, and via drive-up windows at all locations.

Middletown State Bank distinguishes itself through personalized community service, with specific emphasis on convenience features like night depositories at all branches, safe deposit boxes ranging from $15–$25 annually, drive-up banking, and extended Friday hours at the Latham branch (until 6 p.m.). The bank also offers wire transfer services (Latham branch only) and faxing services at all locations, plus financial planning tools to support customer decision-making.

As a traditional community bank, Middletown State Bank is best suited for customers seeking local, in-person banking relationships and those living or operating businesses in central Illinois. However, the bank's service offerings and technology features appear limited compared to larger regional or national banks, and specific product details (interest rates, loan terms, minimum deposits) are not disclosed on the website. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Wire transfers (Latham branch only; $12 incoming/$22 outgoing)
pros: Three convenient branch locations across Illinois with extended Saturday hours (8 a.m.–Noon drive-up)
Personalized community banking with 100+ years of local history and established relationships
Night depository service at all branches for after-hours deposits and document submission
Safe deposit boxes available at affordable annual rates ($15–$25) at all locations
Extended Friday hours at Latham branch (8 a.m.–6 p.m.) for working customers
Wire transfer services available with posted fee structure ($12 incoming, $22 outgoing)
Full-service offerings including personal loans, business checking, online and mobile banking
cons: Limited service area restricted to three small Illinois towns (Middletown, Greenview, Latham)
Wire transfer service only available at Latham branch, not all locations
No interest rates, APRs, minimum deposit requirements, or loan terms disclosed on website
Latham branch has restricted hours (8 a.m.–3 p.m. Mon–Wed, Noon Thu) compared to other branches
Limited technology visibility; no information about mobile app features, online banking capabilities, or digital security certifications
best_for: Residents of Middletown, Greenview, or Latham, Illinois seeking convenient local banking
Small business owners in central Illinois needing personalized business checking and loan services
Customers who value in-person relationships and prefer community banks over large national chains
Individuals needing safe deposit boxes and after-hours deposit services
diagnosis: Middletown State Bank is best for customers living in or operating businesses within its three-branch Illinois service area who value community relationships and personalized service. The primary caveat is that the bank's service area is geographically limited and much information about specific products, rates, and terms is not publicly available on the website, requiring direct contact with branch staff.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
